avocado.pollination_note: A/B flowering types and compatible cultivars can improve fruit set.

sour_cherry.pollination_note: Cultivar-dependent; many sour cherries are self-fertile, but yields can improve with compatible cultivars.

fig.cultivation_note: Hardiness is cultivar-dependent; hardy selections may overwinter in protected Zone 6 sites.

sea_buckthorn_.pollination_note: Dioecious; needs male and female plants for fruit.

hardy_kiwi.pollination_note: Usually requires male and female plants; one male can pollinate multiple females.

saskatoon_serviceberry.pollination_note: Often self-fertile, but better yields with another cultivar.

honeyberry.pollination_note: Requires two compatible varieties or cultivars for good fruit set.

miracle_berry.cultivation_note: Tropical/subtropical and frost-sensitive; container candidate outside warm zones.

asian_pear.pollination_note: Many varieties need or benefit from compatible pear pollination.

jujube.pollination_note: Cultivar-dependent; some are self-fertile and some benefit from another cultivar.

beach_plum.cultivation_note: Coastal, sandy, drought-tolerant plum; distinct from honeyberry/haskap.

che_fruit.pollination_note: Cultivar-dependent; some plants are dioecious, while some named cultivars may be self-fertile.

coconut.pollination_note: Wind and insect pollinated; tropical and frost-sensitive.

tropical_almond.cultivation_note: Check local suitability and spread concerns before planting in sensitive coastal or island ecosystems.

papaya.pollination_note: Sex expression and cross-pollination needs vary by cultivar.

banana.pollination_note: Usually propagated vegetatively; fruiting depends on cultivar and climate.

plantain.pollination_note: Usually propagated vegetatively; fruiting depends on cultivar and climate.

guava.cultivation_note: Check local spread concerns before planting in sensitive island ecosystems.

surinam_cherry.cultivation_note: Check local spread concerns before planting.

cacao.cultivation_note: Best as a humid tropical understory crop with wind protection and partial shade.

coffee.cultivation_note: Best as a humid, frost-free understory crop.

chaya.cultivation_note: Cooked leafy green; do not treat as a raw-use recommendation.

cassava.cultivation_note: Use appropriate preparation practices for edible roots; not a raw-use recommendation.

katuk.cultivation_note: Traditional leafy vegetable; use conservative preparation and local guidance.

vanilla.cultivation_note: Humid tropical understory vine; pollination and curing are specialized.
